plans.name: Basic
plans.description: Basic plan with SD streaming on one device at a time.
plans.download_limits_description: You can download videos on 1 phone or tablet at a time with the Basic plan.

plans.name: Standard
plans.description: Standard plan with HD and some 4K titles, plus downloads on multiple devices.
plans.download_limits_description: You can download videos on up to 2 phones or tablets at a time on the Standard plan; download limits also apply per title and per account.

plans.name: Premium
plans.description: Premium plan with 4K Ultra HD and HDR on supported devices, plus maximum download flexibility.
plans.download_limits_description: You can download videos on up to 4 phones or tablets at a time on the Premium plan, with higher limits for total downloadable titles.

article_sections.title: 4K streaming requirements overview
Check your internet speed for 4K
step_texts: On a computer or mobile browser, go to a trusted internet speed test site.
Run the test on the same network your streaming device uses.
Check that your download speed is at least 25 Mbps for consistent 4K streaming.
If your speed is lower, try moving closer to your router or connecting with an Ethernet cable, then run the test again.

article_sections.title: About buffering and poor video quality on smart TVs
Troubleshooting steps for smart TV buffering
step_texts: Pause playback for 30 seconds, then resume the video to see if quality improves.
Restart your smart TV, then reopen the streaming app.
Unplug your modem and router for 30 seconds, plug them back in, and wait for all lights to stabilize.
If possible, connect your TV to the router with an Ethernet cable instead of Wi-Fi.
Try playing a different title to see if buffering is limited to a single movie or show.

article_sections.title: Before you cancel on a web browser
Cancel using a web browser
step_texts: Open a web browser and go to the streaming service website.
Sign in to the account you want to cancel, if you are not already signed in.
Hover over your profile icon and click Account to open your account settings page.
Under Membership & Billing, click Cancel membership.
On the confirmation screen, review your end date and select a reason for canceling (optional).
Click Finish cancellation to confirm. A confirmation message will appear and an email will be sent to you.

article_sections.title: Before you cancel on your TV
Cancel using your TV
step_texts: On your smart TV or TV app, open the streaming app.
From the app home screen, go to Settings or Get Help.
Select Account, then choose Cancel membership or Manage subscription.
Review the details and confirm cancellation when prompted on your TV screen.

article_sections.title: About subtitles and audio on Android
Change subtitles or audio language on Android
step_texts: Open the streaming app on your Android phone or tablet.
Play a movie or episode, then tap the screen to show playback controls.
Tap the speech bubble, audio, or subtitles icon (usually in the upper-right corner).
Under Subtitles, select the subtitle language you want to use, or choose Off to disable subtitles.
Under Audio, select your preferred audio language, if more than one is available.
Tap the X or back arrow to close the menu, then resume playback to confirm the new subtitle or audio language is applied.

article_sections.title: How maturity ratings work
Profile-level controls
step_texts: On a web browser, go to the streaming service website and sign in as the account owner.
Hover over your profile icon and click Account, then scroll to the Profiles & Parental Controls section.
Next to the profile you want to manage (for example, a 13-year-old), click Profile-level controls.
Click the link labeled Open profile settings in a new tab to adjust maturity ratings and other restrictions for that profile.
In the new tab, choose a maturity rating such as Teen or 13+ and save your changes.

article_sections.title: Why set a PIN to restrict changes
Set a PIN to restrict changes
step_texts: Sign in to the account owner profile on a web browser.
Go to Account, then scroll to the Profiles & Parental Controls section.
Next to the profile you want to protect, select Change next to Profile lock or PIN settings.
Create a 4-digit PIN, confirm it, and choose whether the PIN is required to change maturity ratings or profile settings.
Save your changes. The profile will now require the PIN before key settings can be updated.

article_sections.title: When to update your card
Change your payment method online
step_texts: Sign in to your account on the streaming service website.
From the Account page, locate the Membership & Billing section.
Click the button or link labeled Go to payment settings. A new payment settings page will open.
In payment settings, update your card details or select a different saved payment method. If needed, choose United States from the Billing country dropdown.
Review your changes and click Save or Update to apply the new payment method to your subscription.

article_sections.title: How many devices can download on your plan
See which devices are using your download slots
step_texts: Open the streaming app on a phone or tablet that is currently downloaded to your account.
Go to Downloads, then select Manage devices or Manage download devices.
Review the list of devices using download slots and remove any you no longer use to free up space for new downloads.

article_sections.title: Download limits for other plans
Fix error VC_201 on computers
step_texts: Quit your browser or desktop app completely, then reopen it and try playing the video again.
Check that your computer has an active internet connection by opening another website in a new tab.
Clear your browser cache and cookies for the streaming site, then sign in again.
If you still see VC_201, scroll to the bottom of this article and contact support with the error code VC_201 included in your message.

articles.title: 4K streaming requirements and recommended internet speed
articles.summary: Learn what internet speed, hardware, and account settings you need to stream in 4K Ultra HD.
articles.body:
## 4K streaming requirements overview

To watch in 4K Ultra HD, your device, internet connection, and account must all meet certain requirements. This article explains the minimum and recommended internet speeds, compatible devices, and how to confirm you're watching in 4K.

### What you need for 4K
- A plan that includes 4K or Ultra HD streaming
- A 4K-capable TV or monitor connected with HDMI 2.0 or later
- The latest version of the streaming app or a supported browser
- A stable high‑speed internet connection

## Check your internet speed for 4K

Use the steps in the **Check your internet speed for 4K** section of this article to test your connection. In general, we recommend:

- **Minimum 25 Mbps** downstream per 4K stream
- **Higher speeds** if multiple devices are streaming at the same time

If your speed test shows less than 25 Mbps, follow the troubleshooting tips in this article or in the **buffering on smart TVs** help article.

articles.title: Buffering or poor video quality on smart TVs
articles.summary: Fix buffering, freezing, and low video quality when streaming on your smart TV.
articles.body:
## About buffering and poor video quality on smart TVs

If your video pauses to buffer, looks blurry, or switches between resolutions on your smart TV, it usually points to a network or device performance issue. This article walks you through the most common fixes.

Possible causes include:
- Slow or unstable internet connection
- Wi‑Fi interference or weak signal
- Outdated TV firmware or app version
- Temporary issues with our service

## Troubleshooting steps for smart TV buffering

Follow the numbered steps in the **Troubleshooting steps for smart TV buffering** section to try quick fixes like restarting your TV and modem.

If you've tried all of the steps and still have problems, scroll to the bottom of this article and select **Contact support about this issue**. On the contact form, choose **Streaming quality** as the topic and describe when the buffering happens (for example, *"happens every evening around 8 PM"*).

articles.title: Cancel your subscription on a web browser
articles.summary: Learn how to cancel your streaming subscription using a web browser on your computer or mobile device.
articles.body:
## Before you cancel on a web browser

You can cancel your subscription at any time from your Account page in a web browser. After canceling, you can keep watching until the end of your current billing period.

We’ll save your profiles, viewing history, and preferences for a limited time in case you decide to come back.

## Cancel using a web browser

Follow the numbered steps in the **Cancel using a web browser** section of this article:

1. Go to the streaming service website in a web browser.
2. Sign in with the account you want to cancel.
3. Open your **Account** page.
4. Under **Membership & Billing**, choose **Cancel membership**.
5. Review the details and confirm your cancellation.

If you signed up through a third party (such as an app store or TV provider), you may see a message telling you to cancel directly with that provider instead.

articles.title: Cancel your subscription on your TV app
articles.summary: Cancel your subscription directly from the streaming app on your smart TV or streaming device.
articles.body:
## Before you cancel on your TV

Some devices let you cancel your subscription directly from the streaming app. If you don’t see a cancellation option, follow the instructions in **Cancel your subscription on a web browser** instead.

## Cancel using your TV

Use the steps in the **Cancel using your TV** section of this article to cancel from a supported TV app. The exact wording of menu items can vary slightly depending on the device manufacturer.

After cancelling on your TV, you’ll see an on‑screen confirmation, and we’ll send a confirmation email to your account email address.

articles.title: Change subtitles or audio language on Android
articles.summary: Turn subtitles or closed captions on or off and change the audio language in the Android app.
articles.body:
## About subtitles and audio on Android

You can customize subtitles, captions, and audio language while playing most TV shows and movies on your Android phone or tablet.

Supported options depend on the title and your region. Some titles may not offer every language.

## Change subtitles or audio language on Android

Follow the detailed steps in the **Change subtitles or audio language on Android** section of this article to:

- Turn subtitles or captions on or off
- Choose a different subtitle language
- Switch to a different audio language (if available)

Once you save your choice, we’ll try to use the same subtitle and audio language for future titles when possible.

At the top of this article you can also switch the article language. For example, choose **Spanish (ES)** to view these instructions in Spanish.

articles.title: Set parental controls by maturity rating
articles.summary: Choose which TV shows and movies each profile can watch by setting maturity ratings like Little Kids, Older Kids, or Teen/13+.
articles.body:
## How maturity ratings work

Parental controls let you choose the highest maturity rating that each profile on your account can watch. Titles above that rating will be hidden from that profile.

For example, you might:
- Set a **Kids** profile to *Older Kids* content only
- Set a **teen** profile (around age 13) to *Teen/13+* content

## Profile-level controls

Use the steps in the **Profile-level controls** section of this article to:

- Open the **Profiles & Parental Controls** section of your Account
- Select a profile (for example, a 13‑year‑old)
- Click **Profile-level controls**
- Use the **Open profile settings in a new tab** link to adjust maturity ratings and other restrictions

You can combine maturity ratings with a profile PIN to prevent changes to these settings. See **Set a PIN to restrict changes** in the related articles section.

articles.title: Set a PIN to restrict changes to profiles and parental controls
articles.summary: Create a PIN so that only you can change maturity ratings or profile settings.
articles.body:
## Why set a PIN to restrict changes

A profile PIN helps prevent kids or other household members from changing maturity ratings, profile settings, or switching into profiles meant for adults.

You can:
- Require a PIN to change parental control settings
- Require a PIN to open a specific profile

## Set a PIN to restrict changes

Follow the instructions in the **Set a PIN to restrict changes** section of this article to create or update a PIN.

After you save your PIN, make sure you remember it or store a secure hint. If you forget your PIN, you’ll need access to the account owner’s email to reset it.

articles.title: Update the card used for your subscription
articles.summary: Change the payment card for your monthly subscription or update an expired card.
articles.body:
## When to update your card

You may need to update your card if:
- Your current card has expired
- You received a replacement card with a new number
- You want to switch to a different card or payment method

## Change your payment method online

Use the steps in the **Change your payment method online** section of this article to open **payment settings**, update your card details, and save your changes.

On the **Payment settings** page, you can also change your **Billing country** (for example, to **United States**) if you’ve moved to a new country and are using a valid payment method from that country.

articles.title: How many devices can download videos on my plan?
articles.summary: Find out how many phones or tablets can download videos at the same time on each plan.
articles.body:
## How many devices can download on your plan

Your subscription plan limits how many phones or tablets can have downloads at the same time.

- **Basic plan:** 1 device
- **Standard plan:** 2 devices
- **Premium plan:** 4 devices

If you’ve reached your limit, you’ll see a message when you try to download on another device.

## See which devices are using your download slots

Follow the steps in the **See which devices are using your download slots** section to review and manage devices.

In the **Related articles** section, select **See download limits for other plans** to compare all plan options in more detail.

articles.title: See download limits for other plans
articles.summary: Compare download limits for Basic, Standard, and Premium plans, including how many devices and titles you can download.
articles.body:
## Download limits for other plans

This article compares download limits across all available plans.

For each plan, you’ll see:
- How many devices can store downloads at the same time
- How many titles you can download at once
- Any additional restrictions that may apply

Use this information together with **How many devices can download videos on my plan?** to decide whether switching plans better fits your household’s download needs.

articles.title: Fix error VC_201 on computers
articles.summary: Troubleshoot error code VC_201 when streaming from a web browser or desktop app on your computer.
articles.body:
## What error VC_201 means

Error **VC_201** usually indicates a temporary connection problem or an issue loading the video player in your browser or desktop app.

## Fix error VC_201 on computers

Work through the steps in the **Fix error VC_201 on computers** section of this article. After each step, try playing your video again.

If you still see VC_201 after trying all the steps, scroll to the bottom of this article and choose the fastest contact option (such as **Chat**). Make sure the contact form includes the error code **VC_201** so our agents can help more quickly.

articles.title: Set up and sign in on your smart TV
articles.summary: Install the app, link your account, and start watching on a supported smart TV.
articles.body:
## Install the app on your TV

Open your TV’s app store, search for the streaming app, and install it.

## Sign in and start watching

Open the app, sign in with your account email and password, then choose a profile and start watching.

articles.title: Create and manage profiles on your account
articles.summary: Add, edit, and delete profiles so everyone in your household has their own recommendations and settings.
articles.body:
## About profiles

Profiles let each person in your household have their own personalized experience.

You can create kids profiles, teen profiles, and adult profiles with different settings and maturity levels.

articles.title: Understand your monthly bill
articles.summary: See how we calculate your monthly charge and where to find your invoices.
articles.body:
## What’s included in your bill

Your monthly bill includes your base plan price plus any applicable taxes. If your plan or billing country changes mid‑cycle, you may see partial charges or credits.
